name: Fasten Health
researched_as: Fasten Health personal/family health record app
what: Self-hosted personal and family health record aggregator for individuals, not an enterprise healthcare platform.
solves: Consumer-controlled aggregation of personal medical records and healthcare documents.
users: Individuals/families, not businesses building customer operations.
fit: Poor fit for Hylono’s current stack. It is a personal health record app rather than an operational platform for a premium wellness company.
runtime: Self-hosted consumer app with healthcare account connectors and record storage.
components: Web app, data import/connectors, storage, auth, and personal-record management.
deploy: Self-hostable, but strategic value for Hylono is low unless the business moves into patient-controlled records.
deps: Storage, auth, healthcare connectors, and data persistence.
apis: Consumer-focused import/connectivity surfaces; not a strong business integration target here.
security: Health records are highly sensitive. Not appropriate to introduce casually in a health-adjacent business stack.
setup_path: Do not prioritize; keep outside the roadmap unless product direction changes dramatically.
env: App settings, storage, connector credentials where relevant.
persistence: Sensitive health records would require strong backup/encryption controls if ever used.
operations: Connector maintenance and sensitive-data governance are the main issues.
failure_points: Confusing a personal-record product with a business operations platform.
verify: Confirm whether any product vision actually requires customer-controlled health-record aggregation.
gdpr: Would involve special-category health data if used.
overlap: Not meaningfully overlapping with current Hylono operational needs.
codex_read_first: Read the business-fit notes; this is likely out of scope.
codex_best_path: Do not implement unless the business model changes toward patient-record handling.
codex_never_assume: Never adopt a health-record system just because the company is health-adjacent.

sources.why: Used to assess version/cadence; potentially outdated.
